name: Trimbakeshwar Shiva Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas in India. Sacred temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located at the source of the Godavari River.

name: Panchavati Ramkund
description: Sacred site where Lord Rama is believed to have bathed during his exile. Center of religious significance in Nashik.

name: Pandavleni Caves
description: Ancient rock-cut Buddhist caves dating back to 3rd century BC. Features intricate carvings and meditation halls.

name: Sula Vineyards
description: India's most famous vineyard offering wine tastings, tours, and the iconic Sula Fest. Scenic views of Gangapur Dam.

name: Saptashrungi Garh
description: Hilltop temple dedicated to Goddess Saptashrungi, surrounded by seven mountain peaks. Requires ropeway or trek.

name: Harishchandragad Fort
description: Ancient hill fortress famous for Konkan Kada cliff. Popular trekking destination with historic temples and caves.

name: Shirdi Sai Baba Temple
description: One of India's most visited pilgrimage sites. Samadhi temple of Sai Baba, visited by millions annually.

name: Goda Ghat
description: Serene ghats along the Godavari River. Perfect for morning walks and experiencing local culture.

name: Sahyadri Farms
description: Experience rural Maharashtra with grape farms, traditional cuisine, and agricultural tours in the Sahyadri foothills.

name: Kumbh Mela Ground
description: World's largest religious gathering venue. Next Kumbh Mela in August 2027. Sacred bathing ghats and temporary city.

name: Samartha Juice Center
description: Famous local spot for fresh sugarcane juice, seasonal fruit juices, and traditional Maharashtrian refreshments.

name: Bhandardara Lake
description: Scenic hill station with Arthur Lake, Wilson Dam, and Ratangad Fort. Perfect for camping and stargazing.

name: Traditional Cuisine
description: Experience authentic Nashik cuisine including Misal Pav, Sabudana Vada, and local Maharashtrian thalis.

name: Heritage Architecture
description: Colonial-era buildings and traditional wadas showcasing Nashik's rich architectural heritage.

name: Taparia Nature Trail
description: Peaceful nature walk through local flora and fauna. Bird watching spot and picnic area.

name: Anjaneri Hills
description: Birthplace of Lord Hanuman according to mythology. Popular trekking destination with Hanuman Temple at summit.

name: Budha Halwai
description: Iconic sweet shop famous for traditional Nashik sweets including Jalebi, Basundi, and seasonal delicacies.

name: Gangapur Dam
description: Picturesque dam with boating facilities and garden. Popular picnic spot with sunset views over water.
